codeReview.summary: Pass. The original rerun failure was caused by `tests/e2e/sync-workflow.test.ts` using `test-skill`, which does not exist on `shuvtest`, so activation could not produce a valid target and `getSkillStatus` remained inactive. The fix switches the scenario to the cross-host `adapt` skill (`tests/e2e/sync-workflow.test.ts:51`) and retains assertions that directly cover the failure mode: status must become `active` after activation (`tests/e2e/sync-workflow.test.ts:181`), the symlink target must resolve to the expected repo path (`tests/e2e/sync-workflow.test.ts:205-206`), and a second activation must return `alreadyInState=true` (`tests/e2e/sync-workflow.test.ts:264`). Those checks match the `SkillOps` semantics in `packages/skill-ops/src/live.ts:517-524` and `packages/skill-ops/src/live.ts:871-873`, so I did not find any remaining blocking correctness defect in the current implementation.

summary: Pass: the fix replaces the Linux-missing `test-skill` with cross-host `adapt`, and the current sync-workflow assertions now directly validate the activation, status, and idempotency behavior that previously failed.
